mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as market participants reprice growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) of more than 100,000 jobs over 3 months signals labor market deterioration that precedes broader economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100,000 jobs over 3-month rolling window
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite payroll declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.10 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) remain above 1,300 thousand units, indicating robust consumer demand and construction activity. This joint expansion signals healthy aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5% AND HOUST > 1,300 thousand units
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with sustained demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances despite joint demand strength, or interaction coefficient between RSAFS and HOUST is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, indicating simultaneous supply-side weakness and labor market softening. This combination signals stagflationary pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity risk premium expands.
regime_hint: stagflationary regime with supply constraints and labor softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of INDPRO and UNRATE on forward returns is not statistically significant (interaction p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates below 3.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, signaling that monetary policy has successfully contained inflation without requiring further tightening. This regime shift produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as terminal rate expectations stabilize.
regime_hint: post-tightening disinflation with policy rates held steady
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite disinflation, or the interaction between inflation deceleration and elevated rates does not improve forward return predictability (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized in the most recent quarter, signaling robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth. This always-on trend mechanism produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as long as growth momentum persists.
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in > 55% of periods with GDPC1 growth > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, signaling labor market slack that reduces wage pressure and supports profit margins. This threshold-triggered mechanism produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as inflation expectations decline.
regime_hint: post-tightening regime with labor market cooling
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite elevated unemployment, or correlation between UNRATE levels and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month while consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 3.5% year-over-year, indicating supply-side expansion without inflationary pressure. This combination signals healthy productivity growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 0.3% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 3.5%
regime_hint: goldilocks regime with supply growth and contained inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite supply expansion and low inflation, or the interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).
